title: Fix 'for' loop with outside variable reference
body:
Verilator 5.019 devel rev v5.018-1-g839315b27 is crashing with an Internal Error on SystemVerilog code that works fine in Verilator 5.011 devel rev v5.010-114-g4b7b185d0 and earlier releases that I had been using until recently. I am running on Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS. 

See below for lzc.sv that triggers the failure, and for a debug trace of the failure.

This leading zero counter is a bit unusual in coding style because it is parameterized, but simulates properly with Questa and synthesizes properly with Design Compiler.

David Harris

```
module lzc #(parameter WIDTH = 1) (
  input  logic [WIDTH-1:0]            num,    // number to count the leading zeroes of
  output logic [$clog2(WIDTH+1)-1:0]  ZeroCnt // the number of leading zeroes
);

  integer i;
  
  always_comb begin
    i = 0;
    while ((i < WIDTH) & ~num[WIDTH-1-i]) i = i+1;  // search for leading one
    ZeroCnt = i[$clog2(WIDTH+1)-1:0];
  end
endmodule

harris@vlsi:~/cvw/sim$ verilator --lint-only ../src/generic/lzc.sv --debug
Starting Verilator 5.019 devel rev v5.018-1-g839315b27
```
